title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: A coronary artery bypass graft, or cabbage, is performed to improve circulation to the

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: heart muscle in people with severe coronary artery disease.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: In this procedure, a healthy artery or vein from another part of the body is connected

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: or grafted to the blocked coronary artery.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: A grafted artery or vein bypasses the blocked portion of the coronary artery, carrying oxygen

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: rich blood to the heart muscle.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: One or more coronary arteries may be bypassed during a single operation.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: During a conventional bypass surgery, your surgeon begins by making an incision in the skin

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: over your breastbone or sternum.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: He or she will then cut the sternum and move your ribcage in order to get to your heart.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: Throughout the procedure, your circulatory system will be connected to a cardiopulmonary

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: bypass pump or heart lung machine.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: This machine temporarily performs the functions of your heart and lungs during the surgery,

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: allowing your heart to be stopped while the surgeon soars the grafts into place.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: One of two blood vessels is typically used for the grafts, the internal thoracic artery,

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: in the chest or the saffinous vein in the leg.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: For the internal thoracic artery graft, your surgeon will leave the upper end attached

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: to the subclavian artery and divert the lower end from your chest wall to your coronary

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: artery just beyond the blockage.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: Your surgeon will then sew the graft into place.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: For the saffinous vein graft, your surgeon will suture one end to the aorta and the

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: other end to the narrowed artery just beyond the blockage.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: With the grafts securely in place, your surgeon will use electrical signals to restore the

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: heart beat and attach a temporary pacemaker to the heart.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: Once your heart is again beating normally, the heart lung machine will be disconnected.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: Your surgeon will wire the breastbone back together and suture the skin incision closed.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: A temporary drainage tube will be placed through the skin beneath the incision.
